role_title: Senior Technical Program Manager, Product & Rider Loyalty

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week (Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays)

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in a customer-facing product environment
Proven ability to operate effectively and autonomously across multiple teams
Compensation range $148,000–$185,000 base consistent with Senior IC at Lyft

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in a customer-facing product environment, leading cross-functional teams to deliver complex product and platform initiatives iteratively, with multiple dependencies and constraints, in a highly dynamic and agile environment.
Proven ability to operate effectively and autonomously across multiple teams in situations of extreme ambiguity, with only high-level direction.
Experience as an embedded TPM partnering within product engineering teams, with the flexibility to shift into generalist capacity supporting multiple teams concurrently.
Experience building roadmaps, release plans, and program plans with a thorough understanding of dependency management.
Able to communicate highly technical problems and solutions at all levels — from individual contributors to C-level executives.
Able to influence, negotiate, and inspire others in a matrixed environment; excellent organization, planning skills, and attention to detail.
Experience compiling and presenting business and technology options, backed by data and experimentation, to decision makers.

scope.surface: Rideshare — Partnerships, Loyalty and Payments organization; singular loyalty offering for riders and drivers
scope.business_model: B2C rideshare platform, loyalty and retention product
primary_metrics_inferred: KPI ownership and measurement across loyalty product programs
Delivery velocity and iterative feature launches
core_responsibilities: Lead cross-functional product initiatives from ideation through planning, execution, launch, and post-launch

Provide hands-on support across all phases of the software development lifecycle, including project planning, resource allocation, progress tracking, risk mitigation, KPI ownership and measurement, and leadership communications
Partner with engineering, product, data science, design, operations, and other business functions to build highly collaborative teams
Drive loyalty and retention product programs, working closely with product and engineering to define success metrics, prioritize roadmap investments, and accelerate delivery
Lead regulatory and compliance-driven product initiatives, navigating ambiguity and coordinating across legal, policy, product, and engineering stakeholders

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned alongside bonus

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($148,000–$185,000 base) plus equity and bonus
Clear product surface and ownership area (Partnerships, Loyalty and Payments within Rideshare)
Well-defined hybrid schedule (3 days/week) with specific days named — no ambiguity about office expectations
Flexibility to work remotely up to 4 weeks per year
Comprehensive benefits listed including 18 weeks paid parental leave and 401(k) match
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not addressed — unclear for candidates requiring sponsorship
No nice-to-haves listed, making it harder to understand what differentiates candidates
Role spans regulatory/compliance-driven work as well as loyalty product — broad scope may indicate under-staffing or shifting priorities

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp range, and years required)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: Compensation note says 'San Francisco area' but does not specify whether other office locations have different ranges — a recruiter note suggests ranges vary by location.
JD mentions 'hybrid' role and '#Hybrid' tag; no metadata conflict detected, treating as hybrid with 3 days/week in-office requirement.
